abilityRationale: Strength drives your attacks and damage; Constitution keeps you standing (and boosts HP every level); Dexterity helps your no-armor AC and initiative.

subclasses.rationale: The simplest barbarian — more rage-fueled damage, no extra rules to track.
Trades some raw damage for a free natural weapon and extra utility, still simple to run.

abilityRationale: Charisma powers your spells and most of your class features; Dexterity and Constitution round out survivability since Bards wear light armor at best.

subclasses.rationale: More magical secrets and expertise — the most flexible, hardest-to-go-wrong pick.
More build-around, but very strong support/utility if you want to lean into that.

abilityRationale: Wisdom drives your spellcasting; Constitution keeps you alive in melee range; Strength (or Dexterity instead) depends on whether you fight up close.

subclasses.rationale: The best healer in the game and very forgiving to play — a great first Cleric.
Strong all-rounder: darkvision-sharing, temp HP for the party, and solid spells.

abilityRationale: Wisdom drives spells and Wild Shape DCs; Constitution and Dexterity both matter since you'll spend time both as yourself and as a beast.

subclasses.rationale: A summon-based healer/damage-dealer that adds a lot without much extra complexity.
A melee-leaning Druid with a fun necrotic damage trick — good if you want to fight in Wild Shape less.

abilityRationale: Strength (or Dexterity for a finesse/ranged build) drives your attacks; Constitution is huge for a class that gets the most hit points and the most attacks.

subclasses.rationale: The lowest-maintenance subclass in the game — better crits, nothing to manage.
More powerful and more flexible, with some size-changing and rune tricks, but a bit more to track.

abilityRationale: Dexterity drives your attacks and AC; Wisdom boosts that same AC and your saves; Constitution helps you survive being in melee with light armor.

subclasses.rationale: The most straightforward martial-arts monk — reliable control effects on your unarmed strikes.
Adds real healing to the Monk kit if you want to support as well as punch.

abilityRationale: Strength drives your attacks; Charisma powers your spells, auras, and smites; Constitution keeps a heavily-armored front-liner standing.

subclasses.rationale: Classic 'holy knight' — strong at controlling and disabling a single dangerous enemy.
Leans into mobility and heroic feats of strength — great if you want to be the party's action hero.

abilityRationale: Dexterity drives most Ranger weapon choices and AC; Wisdom powers your spells; Constitution is always worth investing in.

subclasses.rationale: The most reliable damage output and the simplest to play — a safe first pick.
Adds charm/fear utility and a bit more magic — good if you want more than 'shoot things'.

abilityRationale: Dexterity drives your attacks, AC, and most of your skills; Constitution and Wisdom round out survivability and perception.

subclasses.rationale: More item/utility tricks and the best at using magic items — flexible, easy to use.
Psychic blades you can throw and recall, plus some short-range teleportation — a fun, mobile pick.

abilityRationale: Charisma is your entire spellcasting engine; Constitution matters a lot since Sorcerers have the fewest hit points of any full caster.

subclasses.rationale: Extra hit points and AC baked in — the most forgiving Sorcerer subclass for a first try.
Strong support and protection spells layered onto your normal spell list.

abilityRationale: Charisma powers Eldritch Blast and all your spells; Constitution and Dexterity help you survive with fewer, shorter-recharging spell slots.

subclasses.rationale: Temporary hit points whenever you drop an enemy — very forgiving in a fight.
A safe pocket dimension to retreat to plus a strong expanded spell list.

abilityRationale: Intelligence drives every Wizard spell and the DCs behind them; Constitution and Dexterity cover the gap left by no armor and the fewest hit points of any class.

subclasses.rationale: Lets you cast damaging spells without catching your own allies in them — very beginner-friendly.
A melee-capable Wizard with great AC — good if you want to be in the fight, not just behind it.

abilityRationale: Intelligence drives your spells and infusions; Constitution and Dexterity round out a class that mixes light armor with magic.

subclasses.rationale: Comes with a steel defender companion that tanks and fights alongside you — the most forgiving Artificer pick.
The best healer/support option among the Artificer subclasses.
